name: Tennessee Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Tennessee - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description:
Tennessee Department of Health
Health Related Boards
665 Mainstream Drive
Nashville, TN 37243

renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by birth month) — no CE required for state renewal

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from the degree-granting institution, showing degree and date awarded.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Copy of your current, active Commission on Dietetic Registration credential.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised Practice Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation verifying completion of ACEND-accredited supervised practice hours.

requiredDocuments.name: Passport-Style Photograph
requiredDocuments.description: Recent photo meeting standard passport specifications.

requiredDocuments.name: Criminal Background Check Authorization
requiredDocuments.description: Mandatory background screening as part of the application process.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Tennessee dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial application fee is $140, which includes the state regulatory fee. The CDR exam costs an additional $200. The biennial renewal fee is $90, due by the last day of your birth month.

mainEntity.name: Does Tennessee require continuing education for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tennessee does not mandate state-level continuing education for license renewal. However, to maintain your CDR registration — which is required for state licensure — you must complete 75 continuing professional education units every five years.

mainEntity.name: Can I practice in Tennessee while waiting for my license?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tennessee offers a temporary permit for $5 for applicants who have CDR authorization to test. This permit allows practice while you await your exam results, provided your application is complete and on file with the Board.

mainEntity.name: Does Tennessee have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tennessee has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Tennessee?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Tennessee while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
